vocab_5ba5420b.definition: Exist; occur; used as an auxiliary verb

vocab_d8f19d0a.definition: Open to more than one interpretation

vocab_2627f608.definition: Lasting for a very short time

review_history.expected: Exist; occur; used as an auxiliary verb

grammar_sva_1.name: Third Person Singular -s
grammar_sva_1.description: Add -s to verbs in third person singular
grammar_sva_1.explanation: In present tense, third person singular (he, she, it) requires the verb to end in -s or -es.

grammar_sva_2.name: Plural Subjects
grammar_sva_2.description: Plural subjects take base form verbs
grammar_sva_2.explanation: Plural subjects use the base form of the verb without -s.

grammar_art_1.name: Indefinite Article A/An
grammar_art_1.description: Use 'a' before consonant sounds, 'an' before vowel sounds
grammar_art_1.explanation: The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on the SOUND, not the spelling. Use 'an' before vowel sounds.

grammar_art_2.name: Definite Article The
grammar_art_2.description: Use 'the' for specific or previously mentioned nouns
correct_examples: The sun rises in the east.
I saw a dog. The dog was brown.
incorrect_examples: I saw dog. Dog was brown.
grammar_art_2.explanation: Use 'the' when referring to something specific, unique, or previously mentioned.

grammar_tense_1.name: Present Continuous
grammar_tense_1.explanation: Present continuous requires the auxiliary verb (am/is/are) + verb-ing.

grammar_tense_2.name: Simple Past Regular Verbs
grammar_tense_2.description: Add -ed to regular verbs for past tense
grammar_tense_2.explanation: Regular verbs form the past tense by adding -ed. Some verbs double the final consonant.

grammar_tense_3.name: Present Perfect
grammar_tense_3.explanation: Present perfect connects past to present. Use 'have' with I/you/we/they, 'has' with he/she/it.

grammar_cond_1.name: First Conditional
correct_examples: If it rains, I will stay home.
If she comes, we will be happy.
If you study, you will pass.
incorrect_examples: If it will rain, I stay home.
If she come, we will be happy.
If you will study, you pass.
grammar_cond_1.explanation: First conditional: If + present simple, will + infinitive. Used for real future possibilities.

grammar_cond_2.name: Second Conditional
correct_examples: If I had money, I would buy a car.
If she were here, she would help.
If they knew, they would tell us.
incorrect_examples: If I have money, I would buy a car.
If she was here, she would help.
If they know, they would tell us.
grammar_cond_2.explanation: Second conditional: If + past simple, would + infinitive. Used for hypothetical situations.

grammar_err_1.name: Their/There/They're
grammar_err_1.description: Distinguish between their, there, and they're

grammar_err_3.name: Its/It's
grammar_err_3.explanation: 'Its' = possessive form of it, 'It's' = it is or it has

grammar_pass_1.name: Passive Voice Formation
grammar_pass_1.description: be + past participle forms passive voice
correct_examples: The book was written by her.
grammar_pass_1.explanation: Passive voice: subject + be + past participle (+ by agent). Focus is on the action, not the doer.
